channel.title: Comments for page "Assistance for the Boss Lady"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Assistance for the Boss Lady"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While this tale suggest that Isabel is tall and skinny adult who simply act like a child, somehow I always visualize her as a little girl.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I couldn't help but read this in Pinkie Pie's voice. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've had quite a chuckle over this line "a monopoly on the local freckle market'. It is now my new favorite way of referring to some with a buttload of freckles.

Fantastical whimsy and loved to see what came of the "new" Dr.Wondertainment after the events of
this tale, including what challenges she might be facing from her predecessor.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just wanted to congratulate you on

She was shortish and plumpish4, with brownish coppery hair kept back in a bun, and a monopoly on the local freckle market.

…which is the most Pratchettesque thing I've read on the site in a couple of years.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really wasn't expecting to enjoy this as much as I did. I actually burst out laughing at the end, when the dogs carry her out of her office while she's laughing maniacally.

Well done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this Wondertainment, it's like that one Johnny Depp Willy Wonka that everyone but me hates.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The corgi waddled into the office, balancing a bucket of ice cream on his head, followed by Jeremy and Jeremy, on and on down a line that stretched out of the office door, a little corgi conga conveyor line.

5. Pronounced la-bor-a-tory. A lab-ro-tory is a dog that spins around.

THAT WAS FUNNY AND AWESOME
Also Jeremy is awesome I want one

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Anybody else getting vibes of Haruhi in Isabel? It's probably just me.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: Aw shit son one hundreeeeeeed.

"There was another long, awkward silence. This time it was hippo-shaped."

+1 right there. Can't wait to read the rest. Grats on the hilarity!

item.title: I love this
item.content:encoded: A corgi ice-cream heist? Dr.Wondertainment hating micro transactions? +1 for you, good sir

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Infinite +1s for the image of corgis pulling an ice cream heist. Unfortunately due to mathematical and ToS restrictions, all but one must be mapped on the imaginary plane.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The controversial bee joke is no problem for me, seeing as it's just as funny when it's not read as a reference.

Yeah, same here. I read right over it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I hope she kicks the Factory's ass.

dis

(this is better than it has any right to be, it's great)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Superb.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
reference to Zoroastrian astrology

This is a bit late, but I have downvoted things in the past for innaccurate representations of Achaemenid civilization.

Because, honestly who confuses the Shahanshah with a "Caliph!?" Who!?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Footnote 5 was a great parting shot. The entire article was hilarious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really? I swear there was another one I downvoted because Wondertainment was too lolrandom.

Maybe I just don't know what I like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First one? This is the first one.

And that makes perfect sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"No, it doesn't make any sense"

Welcome to the SCP Foundation, where nobody gives a crap.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't remember what the first one was called and now I want to reverse my downvote on it because I just realized Wondertainment is Mrs. Frizzle.

No, it doesn't make any sense, shut up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was just delightful on so many levels.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Years of exposure to Terry Pratchett have made me a sucker for a good footnote gag. +1'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Scary thing is, if this was a bit darker in tone, his song "Lament for a Toy Factory" could fit rather well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How did you sneak bees into a tale? You need to be stopped before you destroy us all.

I'll level with you: I didn't think the last one deserved a sequel, and I read most of this thinking it wasn't that great. But between "the Factory is butts", the ice cream flavors, and "the Factory is bees", I had to upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You used bees…and you made it work. Take my +1 you glorious bastard. As for Dr. Wondertainment characterized…I'm impressed! It was very well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a version of Dr Wondertainment that I can see as existing in the same world as Ghost Sign. Which is fantastic.

"Shut up painting of Dad! You aren't my real dad!" may be my favorite line of dialogue ever.

The controversial bee joke is no problem for me, seeing as it's just as funny when it's not read as a reference.

In conclusion, shit was fly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And strictly speaking, the only actionable or malicious sort of downvote would be one where someone were to say, for example, "I don't like the way Pata H complains about other people's downvoting habits, so I'm going to downvote everything they ever write because I've decided I don't like them." That would definitely be stupid and petty, and against Site Rules. Just about everything short of that (including all the ones Gaffney mentions above) are perfectly allowed. A downvote is an expression by a reader that what is being written does not meet up to his or her expectations of what should be on the site or on the page. That might include typos, use of "amnesiac", or reference to Zoroastrian astrology.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know several pettier ones off the top of my head. ;)

EDIT: It doesn't really help that he didn't downvote. People are allowed to vote as they please so long as it's based on the article itself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Except for the part where I didn't downvote, so there's that.

If it had said "the Factory is cakes and the cakes are lies," my feelings would have been the same. I don't like unfunny, played-out references being shoehorned into stuff, as it usually feels forced and throws off the story for me. It might be "petty," but I'm not getting "stupid" or "smug." A stupid reason for downvoting (or neutral voting, as the case may be) would be "you used the letter 'r' too many times" while smug would be, I dunno, "needs moar Flaubert references, pleb."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
“No. The Factory is bees, here.”

You sunuvabitch. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Papal Papaya™, The Horizon Initiative's #1 Approved Frozen Delicacy! Now with 100% All Natural Zeal!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, it isn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is the stupidest, most petty, and smug downvote reason ever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huh, upvote added for characterizing Dr. Wondertainment and being kind of interesting in doing it, too.

Huh, upvote removed after seeing yet another "lol bees" reference (a.k.a. the "your mom"/"that's what she said" of the Foundation).

Looking forward to more, not looking forward to stupid references for the sake of stupid references.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I never even entertained the idea of characterizing Dr Wondertainment. I mean I've never been opposed to the idea, per se, but I sorta liked the nebulous undefined nature of whether there even was a doctor, or if they were human, or what their deal was.

But now there's this. And it's everything I could ever have wanted Dr Wondertainment to be. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're damn skippy. Doctor Steel is but an avatar of Wondertainment! We shall be its Toy Soldiers, to bring forth a new age of Fun, Joy and Whimsy!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll be honest with you djoric, I'd have given you a +1 just for the corgis alone. The rest of the story was just the sweet sweet icing on the corgi cake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wish I could upvote this twice.

It's just so whimsical!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hope she kicks the Factory's ass.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As I said in chat, this is a phenomenal tale that comes closest to my actual interpretation of Wondertainment insofar. Well done, you've earned my +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You utter motherfucker. You had to do it. You just had to do it. You had to characterize Doctor Wondertainment. And because of that, I've coughed up a lung due to laughing so much. I'm dying now. Goodbye.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
THE THING HAS BEEN DONE.

e: fixed typo in title.

ee: Also many thanks to those in chat (you know who you are) who came up with the names for both the ice cream and dad.
